comments.title: (no subject)
comments.content:
There are 15 named deities in the Celtic Golden Dawn system, or 14 if you don't count the OIW. You've talked elsewhere about how it's best to approach a specific pantheon. Are the gods associated with the spheres of the Tree of Life plus the four elemental powers enough to cover every area of life wherein one might need the aid of a divine power? And if not, what other deities are either part or close enough to the Welsh druid pantheon to be called upon?

To be clear, I'm asking specifically about a devotional, rather than, or in addition to, a magical, relationship to the gods.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: Those fourteen deities are a good workable pantheon, but you can also add in the divine or semidivine figures from the Mabinogion, as well as the ancient Celtic deities known from Roman inscriptions, to expand the list into the middle two digits.

comments.title: Questions
comments.content:
Greetings JMG,

I'm curious about the properties of iron; as I had a mentor a while back that warned me not to cook magic related things in steel or iron as it would drain away their energy. I believe you had commented that the anti-glamour spell that you shared needed a point or bladed object of steel or iron. Experimenting, I haven't noticed a loss of energy from water that I draw from a spring near me when I boil it to sanitize it. Am I missing something here? Contemplation on the subject gave me the impression that contact with steel and iron drifts the energy towards mars like qualities.

I've been trying to juggle daily practice with holiday travel and the presence of my kids. Is it ok to perform rituals like the lesser banishing of the pentagram in space that belongs to other people if they are ok with it, even if they don't entirely understand what it is for or what it does?

When I have my children around, if I do my practice in the morning, no matter how early I get up I get about half way through drawing pentagrams and they wake up. They don't generally do this in the evening after they go to bed, but it feels better for me to do that practice in the morning. Well, actually, I am less prone to falling asleep during meditating in the morning... Is it ok to do it at wildly irregular times of day? My impression from a habit perspective is 'no' but I was hoping for your insight.


Thank you for your consideration,
-Truly

replies.title: Re: Questions
replies.content:
1) No, you're not missing anything. To dispel etheric forces, iron or steel has to have a sharp point; you'll get some Martial influence from any iron or steel object, but the water you get from the spring will have absorbed planetary influences from the stone and earth through which it flows.

2) Sure, if they've given consent, you're fine.

3) It's better to do your practices at the same time every day. Are your kids old enough that you can explain to them that you need to do something every morning, and if they wake up, could they play quietly for a bit until you come into the bedroom and let them know you're done?

comments.title: (no subject)
comments.content: You've said before that the future is fluid, but the past is fixed. This would seem to imply something special about the present. As I understand it, from the standpoint of eternity though, there's nothing special about any one time. This would seem to mean that either the fluidity of the future or fixedness of the past is an illusion. Do you know if this is the case, and if so, which one is the illusion?

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: From the standpoint of eternity, time is an illusion; things are neither fixed nor fluid, but a timeless blossoming of potentiality into actuality. "Fixed" and "fluid" are descriptions of our relationship to certain portions of time, not qualities of time itself.

comments.title: déjà vu
comments.content: does occult literature have anything to say about déjà vu's? I would say I get about one a week, should I interpet this in any way?

replies.title: Re: déjà vu
replies.content: You may have some precognitive talent. If so, regular meditation is a safe way to help develop that.

comments.title: Mind connections.
comments.content:
Both my kids and I tend to have fairly strong psychic connections. To the point where I have asked them questions in my mind mid conversation while not
Looking at them and they’ve answered out loud without missing a beat.

Any recommendations and or things I should be on the lookout for for their benefit and talent nurturing?

replies.title: Re: Mind connections.
replies.content: Just make sure they know that a lot of people get freaky about that sort of thing, and they shouldn't talk about it outside the family. Other than that, let their talents ripen naturally.

comments.title: (no subject)
comments.content:
I know someone who's cast Hughes workings. She seems to be slowly drifting further into insanity, but it's a peculiar thing: it seems like people who agree with her politics aren't able to see it, and it's almost as if she's dragging them with her.

Is a contagious insanity a possibility, if so, what could cause it, and does that series of workings carry that risk factor?

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
It's not merely a possibility but a known phenomenon. How else would you describe witch hunts, stock market bubbles, and other examples of "extraordinary popular delusions and the madness of crowds"?

The sort of magical working Hughes is doing is, among other things, brutally vulnerable to that. Because they're trying to bind Trump in general, rather than trying to bind specific actions of his, they've succeeded in binding themselves to Trump. He's become the black hole around which their every thought circles helplessly, and since what you contemplate, you imitate, a great many of them will end up becoming little Trump caricatures, copying him down to the last detail while still insisting that they're fighting him.

comments.title: Heathen Golden Dawn request
comments.content:
Awhile back you made a post asking what else your heathen readers would like included in the system you and Sven are working on.

As far as I know these haven't been suggested yet:

1. A solid memory system.

Aside from the obvious benefits, the symbolism of Odin's ravens huginn and muninn, "thought" and "memory", calls for it in a way:

"Thought and Memory,
my ravens, fly every day
the whole world over.
Each day I fear
that Thought might not return,
but I fear more for Memory."
-Grimnismal Craford translation

As far as I know, the system will already contain your method of discursive meditation or "meditative thinking", including a method of deliberate memory training seems like a good addition.

2. Ritualized physical culture.

Or rather, a ritualized way to begin physical training or competition.

Much like your posts against 'the one true diet' there isn't 'one true' exercise program either. So beyond including some recommended further reading, specific programs aren't what I'm thinking of (though the maxalding stuff is pretty cool).

A ritual or method for 'psyching' yourself up directly before training/competition. Something that would work for an mma fighter about to get in the ring with someone stronger, faster, and at least as skilled; and also work for a seriously injured individual thats about to begin yet another physical therapy session when all the doctors are saying he has a small chance of ever walking again.

The symbolism of this one I imagine is obvious enough. Being willing to fight even when you'll almost certainly lose is something the Norse God's look kindly on.

replies.title: Re: Heathen Golden Dawn request
replies.content: Thanks for this. The physical culture has already been suggested, and we're considering options.
